reason: Setting a GRUB2 boot loader password modifies the bootloader configuration. If the password is forgotten or entered incorrectly, the system may become unbootable, resulting in a lockout. Policy classifies bootloader changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential bootloader lockout; Loss of password prevents system boot; Requires physical console access for recovery; Modifies critical boot configuration
